title: MORIE: Multi-domain Open Research and Inferential Estimation
description: MORIE is an open-source Python and R toolkit for the analysis of Canadian carceral, police, and oversight data. It implements the MRM (McNamara-Ruhela-Medina) framework, including: a 10-estimator per-individual causal ensemble (IPW, AIPW, IRM-DML, PSM, PSM-subclassification, AIPW-SuperLearner, PLR); an aggregate IRR family with a GEE clustering grid for high-cardinality groupings; a Doob chi-square family for aggregate contingency tables; a Mandela Rules classifier that operates at both federal (full operationalization) and provincial (duration-only proxy) levels; a Goffmanian institutional-churn analysis suite; and a stochastic-physics-of-crime stack (Hawkes self-exciting process with exponential, gamma, Weibull, Lomax kernels; reaction-diffusion; Levy flight; urban scaling; Lotka-Volterra). v0.2.0 adds the OTIS/TPS/SIU mrm_* empirical callables (Hill / Gini / Cramer V / Kulldorff space-time scan / LISA quadrants / case-to-decision KM), an ArcGIS REST fetcher for Toronto Police open data, an on-demand scraper for the Ontario SIU Director's Reports, four bundled reference samples, a longitudinal-panel simulator, an animated demo entrypoint (python -m morie.demo), and a GPL-2.0-only signaling layer (SPDX headers, runtime license guard, optional kernel-module + LSM-style audit daemon).
references: Sprott, J. B., & Doob, A. N. (2021, February). Solitary Confinement, Torture, and Canada's Structured Intervention Units. Centre for Criminology & Sociolegal Studies, University of Toronto.

Sprott, J. B., Doob, A. N., & Iftene, A. (2021, May). Do Independent External Decision Makers Ensure that 'An Inmate's Confinement in a Structured Intervention Unit Is to End as Soon as Possible'? Schulich School of Law, Dalhousie University.
Doob, A. N. (2020). Affidavit of Anthony N. Doob (Federal Court of Canada, File T-539-20).
United Nations General Assembly (2015). Standard Minimum Rules for the Treatment of Prisoners (Mandela Rules), A/Res/70/175.
Wallace, M., Turner, J., Matarazzo, A., & Babyak, C. (2009). Measuring crime in Canada: Introducing the Crime Severity Index. Statistics Canada Catalogue no. 85-004-X.
Chernozhukov, V., et al. (2018). Double/debiased machine learning for treatment and structural parameters. Econometrics Journal, 21(1), C1-C68.
VanderWeele, T. J., & Ding, P. (2017). Sensitivity analysis in observational research: Introducing the E-value. Annals of Internal Medicine, 167(4), 268-274.
Goffman, E. (1961). Asylums: Essays on the Social Situation of Mental Patients and Other Inmates. Anchor Books.
notes: MORIE is licensed AGPL-3.0-or-later (strong copyleft; see LICENSE). The optional Linux-kernel adjuncts under kernel-module/ and daemon/ are GPL-2.0-only (kernel ABI requirement). The Ontario SIU scraper is additionally subject to the Ontario open-government licence on the source corpus.
